items.title: Raya spike: homes warned on power, water use
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The public has been urged to use electricity and water prudently during the Hari Raya Aidilfitri period, when consumption of both resources typically rises. The energy transition and water transformation minister said water supply operators are on alert to ensure demand is met, while the National Water Services Commission (Spad) has prepared contingency plans for any unexpected disruptions. The Energy Transition and Water Transformation Ministry (Petra), through Span, is targeting a maximum daily per capita water usage of 160 litres, in line with international benchmarks.

items.title: Diesel supply in Johor restored, monitoring continues
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Diesel supply across Johor has largely stabilised, with most petrol stations now back to normal operations following recent disruptions. She said diesel deliveries were now being carried out in stages to ensure all affected stations receive adequate supply. "The latest updates show that nearly all petrol stations in the affected areas have resumed normal diesel supply," she said.

items.title: Five foreigners arrested, four in Singapore, over Southkey murder
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Police have arrested five foreign men to assist in the investigation into the murder of a man in Southkey on March 14. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the 32-year-old foreign victim was attacked with a sharp weapon about 5am and was later confirmed dead at Sultan Ismail Hospital due to a stab wound to the neck. "Four more suspects were arrested at about 6pm on March 19 with the assistance of the Singapore police," he said in a statement.

items.title: Thunderstorm, heavy rain alert for Selangor, Perak and Sarawak until 3pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in Selangor, Perak and Sarawak until 3pm today. In a statement issued at noon, MetMalaysia said the area expected to be affected in Selangor is Sabak Bernam, while in Perak, the areas are Bagan Datuk and Hilir Perak. In Sarawak, the warning covers Kuching (Kuching), Samarahan, Sri Aman, Betong (Pusa and Betong), Sarikei (Meradong), Sibu (Sibu) and Mukah (Daro, Matu and Dalat).

items.title: Govt eyes labour reforms to address M40 wage stagnation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) is looking into stagnant wage growth among the middle-income (M40) group, as many continue to grapple with the rising cost of living despite a growing economy. BNM Governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said that although the economy is expanding, the benefits may not have been fully realised by the M40. Abdul Rasheed said creating more high-quality jobs would encourage the entire economy to move in tandem, adding that wage increases in one sector would create positive pressure for others to follow suit.

items.title: New wave of Israeli attacks hits Iran despite Trump warning
items.summary: JERUSALEM: Israel launched a fresh wave of attacks on Iran on Friday, a day after President Donald Trump told it not to repeat its strikes on Iranian natural gas infrastructure, which sharply escalated the US-Israeli war on Iran. Bahrain, Kuwait and the United Arab Emirates said they were dealing with missile attacks in the early hours of Friday, following days of Iranian strikes on regional energy infrastructure that has roiled global markets. He said he had told Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu not to repeat the attack on energy infrastructure.

items.title: Three die after Proton X70 collides with Alza on Pan Borneo Highway
items.summary: KUCHING, March 20 — Three people were reportedly killed and five others injured in a crash involving two vehicles on the Pan Borneo Highway along the Serian–Sri Aman route near Kampung Selepong Jinggau today. “Two boys in the same vehicle sustained head injuries and were given initial treatment by the Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) team before being sent to Sri Aman Hospital,” a JBPM spokesperson said. JBPM said the three victims who died were travelling in the Proton X70 — Lau Sie Tung, 70, and two women, Grace Lau Chi Ai, 22, and Lau Yok Leh, 22.

items.title: West Asia conflict could cut global growth by 0.5 points this year – WTO
items.summary: GENEVA: The war against Iran and growing tensions in West Asia are threatening growth in global trade, the World Trade Organisation (WTO) said on Thursday, reported German Press Agency (dpa). In an optimistic scenario without energy price shocks, the WTO sees goods trade growing by 1.9 per cent this year. The WTO put growth in global trade in goods for 2025 at 4.6 per cent.

items.title: MPOB launches used cooking oil price portal to boost circular economy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Palm Oil Board (MPOB) is encouraging industry players and the public to participate actively in the trading of used cooking oil (UCO) as part of efforts to support the nation's circular economy. To ensure transaction transparency and facilitate the buying and selling of UCO, MPOB has provided a used cooking oil price reference portal for the domestic market, which can be accessed for free by all stakeholders, said MPOB in a statement. MPOB director general Datuk Dr Ahmad Parveez Ghulam Kadir said the display of the UCO reference price provides a clearer and reliable price benchmark, thereby supporting the development of a more transparent and inclusive UCO supply chain.

items.title: PLUS expects heavy traffic until night as festive travel peaks ahead of Aidilfitri
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — PLUS Malaysia Berhad reportedly expects traffic on the country’s major highways to remain heavy until late tonight as people travel back to their hometowns for Aidilfitri. Its senior general manager of operations Mohd Yusuf Abdul Aziz said daily traffic still exceeded two million vehicles despite a slight drop from the previous day, according to a report in Berita Harian. He reportedly said that accidents have been handled quickly through cooperation between PLUS, the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) and the Road Transport Department (JPJ).

items.title: Volunteers step in to help struggling family with house cleanup ahead of Aidilfitri
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH: A family in Kampung Durian Daun, Masjid Tanah, received a timely boost ahead of Aidilfitri when volunteers organised a "gotong-royong" to clean and refurbish their poorly maintained home. Housewife Norain Roslan, 33, said she had been unable to manage the rented house properly while caring for six children, aged one to 15, including a five-year-old son with autism. She said that while she wished to work to supplement the family income, her son's condition required constant supervision.

items.title: Indonesia to boost coal output as oil price surge
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia plans to increase coal production in response to rising global oil prices, Coordinating Minister for Economic Affairs Airlangga Hartarto said. He said the move aims to cushion the impact of higher fuel prices and stabilise domestic energy and commodity prices. As of Thursday, brent crude oil prices rose by 6.60 per cent to US$114.47 per barrel.

items.title: Initial volumes of emergency oil reserves already released – IEA
items.summary: PARIS: Initial volumes of oil from emergency reserves have already been available to the market, the International Energy Agency (IEA) said on Thursday, following an agreement by its member countries to release oil from stockpiles, reported Xinhua. The overall release of emergency stocks will largely consist of crude oil, while in Europe contributions will primarily take the form of refined oil products, the IEA said, adding that this will be complemented by additional production from countries in the Americas. The United States and Israel launched massive attacks on Iran on Feb 28, disrupting global shipping, sending oil prices soaring and shaking the global economy.

items.title: Food delivery rider remanded over alleged assault of wife
items.summary: The remand order against the 22-year-old suspect which runs from today to March 23 was issued by Ayer Keroh Court magistrate Uthman Abd Ghani. The remand application was requested by Melaka police Sexual, Women and Children Investigation Division investigating officer Sergeant Siti Norhayati Zainan. Melaka Tengah police chief Assistant Commissioner Christopher Patit said the suspect was arrested at 11am yesterday at a house in Taman Seri Kemendor, Jasin.

items.title: Elderly couple tie the knot after three-month courtship at Penang care home
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 67-year-old man and his 91-year-old bride, both residents of the Silver Jubilee Home in Penang, celebrated their union in a traditional ceremony after a three-month courtship. After getting to know each other over three months, the couple, Ng Chai Hing and Foo Rui Rong, decided to tie the knot in a simple yet heartfelt ceremony at the care home where they first met, China Press reported. "Our sweetest moments are when we hold hands and go for walks outside the home or to buy things together," Foo said.

items.title: Melaka food delivery rider remanded over violent attack on pregnant wife
items.summary: MELAKA, March 20 — A man who beat his pregnant wife on Sunday has been remanded for four days at the Ayer Keroh Magistrate’s Court here, starting today. Yesterday, the police arrested a man who was accused of beating his pregnant wife, in a house in Taman Seri Kemendor, Jasin. Melaka Tengah district police chief ACP Christopher Patit was reported to have said that the 22-year-old suspect was arrested by a team of police officers from the D8 and D11 units, Criminal Investigation Department (CID), Melaka police contingent headquarters (IPK) at around 11 am.

items.title: Brunei eyes launch of stock exchange by 2027
items.summary: BANDAR SERI BEGAWAN: Brunei is planning to establish its own stock exchange by the third quarter of 2027, subject to the completion of an economic ecosystem and market model, a government minister said on Thursday, reported Xinhua. Speaking at the Legislative Council meeting, Haji Mohd Amin Liew Abdullah, minister at the Prime Minister's Office and second minister of finance and economy, said essential components such as auditing systems, the availability of legal advisers and the presence of qualified brokers are needed to support market operations. Haji Mohd Amin Liew added that, as a small market and economy, Brunei faces structural financial market considerations that require careful analysis.

items.title: Vietnam accelerates shift to ethanol fuel as Iran war drives up energy prices
items.summary: HANOI: Vietnam will switch fully to ethanol-blended gasoline earlier than planned as part of its efforts to curb fossil fuel use, a government document showed on Friday, as the war in Iran continues to disrupt global energy supplies. Fuel prices in Vietnam have surged since the US-Israel war on Iran began three weeks ago, with gasoline prices up 50pct and diesel prices rising 70pct, according to data from top fuel trader Petrolimex. The disruption has "caused significant fluctuations in energy prices, creating numerous challenges for countries dependent on energy imports and increasing risks to the energy security of many nations, including Vietnam," the document said.

items.title: Teacher collects 400 Raya cards, including greetings from King and Sultan of Brunei
items.summary: MUAR: A simple handmade greeting from a student with disabilities sparked a lifelong passion for teacher Hafizul Syarafi Mohd Noor, who has since amassed a collection of nearly 400 Hari Raya Aidilfitri cards. Hafizul Syarafi, 36, a visual arts and Malay language teacher at SK Taman Suria, said the hobby began in 2020 when the student sent him a uniquely crafted card. Hafizul Syarafi, who shares his "classic hobby" with his students, believes the sentimental value of physical cards far outweighs digital greetings.

items.title: US stocks fall amid oil price volatility, fading rate cut hopes
items.summary: The S&P 500 sank 0.27 per cent to 6,606.49. The materials and consumer discretionary sectors led the laggards, dropping 1.55 per cent and 0.87 per cent, respectively. Meanwhile, the energy and financials sectors led the gainers, advancing 1.48 per cent and 0.03 per cent.

items.title: Amos Yee to be charged in Singapore after US deportation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Singaporean Amos Yee is expected to be charged today under the country's Enlistment Act, following his deportation from the United States. The 27-year-old was removed from the US on March 19, Singapore time, according to confirmation from the US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agency to The Straits Times. Yee had been in ICE custody since November last year, shortly after his release from an American prison.

items.title: In Aidilfitri greetings, MCCBCHST calls for national unity and social cohesion
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 – The Malaysian Consultative Council of Buddhism, Christianity, Hinduism, Sikhism and Taoism (MCCBCHST) has extended its greetings to the Muslim community as the nation prepares to celebrate Hari Raya Aidilfitri on March 21. In a statement released today, the interfaith council described the festival as a significant time for reflection, gratitude, and renewal. MCCBCHST also said that racial and religious harmony remains the bedrock of Malaysia’s peace, stability, and ongoing progress.

items.title: Narathiwat MP survives pre-dawn shooting outside home, two escorts injured
items.summary: NARATHIWAT: A vehicle carrying Narathiwat member of parliament Kamolsak Leewamo was shot at outside his residence early Friday, shortly after he returned from a parliamentary session in Bangkok. Bacho police chief Wisan Rakmak said Kamolsak escaped unharmed in the incident. Wisan said the incident occurred at about 1am when Kamolsak arrived at his residence from Hat Yai International Airport after attending the parliamentary vote to elect a new prime minister in Bangkok.

items.title: Civil servant scammed of RM160k in 'Future Funds' investment fraud
items.summary: Maran police chief Superintendent Wong Kim Wai said that after a month of correspondence, the suspect offered the victim an investment opportunity via the social media platform, claiming she could assist him in withdrawing profits. Initially, the victim received a return of RM2,000. Throughout the period, the victim never met the suspect and only communicated via WhatsApp and video calls.

items.title: Sixth Malaysian arrested in Singapore over impersonation scams
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 21-year-old Malaysian man has been arrested for his suspected involvement in government official impersonation scams in Singapore, becoming the sixth Malaysian nabbed for similar offences within two weeks. According to The Straits Times, Singapore police in a statement yesterday said they had observed an increasing number of Malaysians travelling to Singapore to assist scam syndicates by collecting cash, gold, and other valuables from victims. Police said the scammers created a sense of urgency and fear by falsely claiming the victim was under investigation for money laundering.

items.title: Months in hospital, but girl's dream of school endures
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: For months, Nur Fatini Husna has remained in a hospital ward, undergoing treatment for acute myeloid leukaemia (AML). In a Facebook post, Deputy Higher Education Minister Adam Adli Abdul Halim said the child's spirit stood out during a recent visit by his special officer to Sultanah Nur Zahirah Hospital in Kuala Terengganu. Even though she has been in a hospital bed for months, Husna never abandoned her studies," he said.

items.title: RTD drones expose emergency lane offenders during festive rush
items.summary: Negri Sembilan RTD deputy director Mohd Hasnor Kutik said the department's state-of-the-art drone monitoring had recorded 135 traffic violations statewide since the start of the Hari Raya Aidilfitri operation (Op HRA) on Monday. "The offences recorded included misuse of emergency lanes, traffic light violations, and queue-cutting. Hasnor said many motorists caught misusing the emergency lane often cited a lack of awareness or confusion over traffic arrangements as their main excuse.

items.title: When the stalls close, the sweeping starts
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The lively atmosphere at the Lintasan Deasoka Ramadan Bazaar, bustling with traders and visitors each evening, gives way to calm once operations end around 7.30pm. A team of cleaners from Kota Kinabalu City Hall's (DBKK) Solid Waste Management Department then moves in, sweeping and collecting rubbish and plastic waste scattered across the site. Cleaning supervisor Wilson Kondo said the task goes beyond routine work, requiring coordination, commitment and strict adherence to schedules.

items.title: Heavy rain and strong winds alert for several districts in Sabah, Sarawak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in Sabah and Sarawak until noon today. In a statement, MetMalaysia said that in Sabah, the areas expected to be affected are Tawau (Tawau and Semporna), while in Sarawak, the warning covers Kuching (Lundu and Kuching), Samarahan (Samarahan and Asajaya), Sarikei (Sarikei and Meradong) and Mukah (Tanjung Manis, Daro, Matu and Dalat). — Bernama

items.title: 75,000 flood Langkawi for Aidilfitri holidays
items.summary: Of the total arrivals since Monday, 18,000 arrived via passenger ferries from Kuala Perlis and Kuala Kedah, while approximately 7,000 used roll-on/roll-off (RoRo) services. Ferry Line Ventures Sdn Bhd (Ferry Line) operations manager Lieutenant Commander (Rtd) Norhafiz Abdul Wahid said 11,230 passengers entered Langkawi through the Kuala Perlis route. "For the Kuala Kedah-Langkawi route, 6,960 local and foreign passengers were recorded across seven trips, including two additional services.

items.title: Scammers use fake KL traffic police letterhead to solicit Raya donations, public warned
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — The Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT) has denied issuing an invitation letter for a Hari Raya Aidilfitri event that also asked for donations from the public. Kuala Lumpur JSPT chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa in a statement today said that his department detected the distribution of the fake letter via WhatsApp application at 5.50 pm yesterday. “Any confirmation can be made directly with the Kuala Lumpur JSPT at 03-2071 9999, the Kuala Lumpur JSPT Hotline at 03-2026 0267/ 0269 or any nearby police station,” he said.

items.title: Homeless, underprivileged camp out for Raya aid at Padang Merdeka
items.summary: Among them is a 36-year-old woman known only as Siti, who travelled from Pasir Puteh with her three children, aged 20 months to 10 years. Despite having to sleep on the pavement, she said desperation drove her to seek funds for her children's festive clothing. "I come to Padang Merdeka every year because donors usually come forward at the end of Ramadan.

items.title: Thailand has 100 days of oil reserves left: PM
items.summary: BANGKOK: Thailand has 100 days of oil reserves left, Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul said on Thursday, as the country tries to manage oil supplies. Oil exports to Laos and Myanmar have also been reduced, he told a news conference. Oil exports to Laos and Myanmar are down 25 per cent and 20 per cent, respectively, from normal levels, Anutin said.

items.title: Raya exodus: LLM says most routes under control except two key highways
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — Traffic flow on several major highways was slow this morning as people began returning to their hometowns to celebrate Aidilfitri tomorrow. Congestion has also been detected on the KL-Karak Expressway heading east, with slow-moving traffic stretching 9.8 kilometres (km) from KM48.1 to KM57.9 (Bukit Tinggi). However, traffic conditions at the location remain manageable.

items.title: 11 killed in anti-cartel operation in Mexico
items.summary: MEXICO CITY: Eleven presumed gunmen were killed in an anti-cartel military operation in northwestern Mexico, officials said on Thursday. The Public Security Secretariat said the operation was carried out by Mexican Marines in the conflict-ridden state of Sinaloa, where government forces raided homes tied to the Sinaloa cartel faction allied with Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada. Mexico has increased arrests of drug traffickers and drug seizures as US President Donald Trump pressures Mexican authorities to crack down on cartels.--AFP © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Government backbenchers hail 50pc toll discount, Raya aid measures
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — The MADANI Government Backbenchers Club (BBC) has welcomed and expressed full support for the government’s immediate measures to safeguard the well-being of the people ahead of the upcoming Hari Raya Aidilfitri. BBC chairman Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa said the approach taken by the government reflects a structured and targeted intervention framework, not only in easing the cost of living but also in strengthening the momentum of the people’s economic recovery. She said the approach encompasses key aspects, including cost of living, social welfare and market stability.

items.title: Sultan of Pahang’s Raya message: Be patient, obey traffic rules, arrive safely
items.summary: KUANTAN, March 20 — The Sultan of Pahang, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, has advised those driving back to their hometowns for Hari Raya Aidilfitri to always be patient and exercise caution when on the road. His Royal Highness, in a post on the Pahang Sultanate Facebook page yesterday, called on road users to always prioritise safety when driving, and to plan their journey properly to avoid stress and fatigue during long trips. “I urge all road users to be patient, obey traffic regulations and ensure your vehicles are in good condition before starting your journey.

items.title: US F-35 stealth fighter hit by suspected Iranian fire
items.summary: WASHINGTON: A US F-35 stealth warplane was hit by suspected Iranian fire and made an emergency landing at American air base in the Middle East, US media reported on Thursday. "The aircraft landed safely, and the pilot is in stable condition," Captain Tim Hawkins, spokesman for US Central Command, said in a statement, without confirming the reports from outlets including ABC and CNN. The United States and Israel launched a massive air campaign against Iran following a major buildup of US military forces – including F-35s – in the region.

items.title: #NSTViral: Car swerves dangerously on highway, sparks safety concerns [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A car has been captured swerving dangerously from side to side along a two-lane highway, raising serious safety concerns for other motorists. In a 50-second video shared on social media, the vehicle repeatedly veers between lanes and brakes abruptly, prompting a driver behind to slow down and keep a safe distance to avoid a potential collision. The user said the incident involved a friend who encountered the driver, describing it as an alleged case of road bullying.

items.title: Gua Musang police report over 30 minor accidents as Raya exodus begins
items.summary: GUA MUSANG, March 20 — More than 30 minor accidents have been reported along the Gua Musang route since yesterday, with most incidents believed to have been due to fatigue from driving for too long and too far. Gua Musang police chief Supt Sik Choon Foo said most of the crashes occurred along the Kuala Lipis and Pahang-Gua Musang highways as well as Federal and state roads in the district. “However, there were no fatal accidents,” he told reporters after the Gua Musang-level Ops Selamat 26 Programme and the breaking of fast event at the Mentara Road Transport Department (JPJ) Enforcement Station here yesterday.

items.title: Aaron Ago Dagang, fellow ministers call for unity and compassion this Aidilfitri
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — Several Cabinet ministers have extended Aidilfitri greetings, emphasising the importance of unity, family values and moderation in Malaysia’s multiracial society. National Unity Minister Datuk Aaron Ago Dagang, in a Facebook post tonight, said Aidilfitri is an ideal platform to strengthen family ties and foster goodwill among people of different faiths. Meanwhile, Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said Aidilfitri serves as a reminder to return to core values such as family, compassion and togetherness.

items.title: MCMC warns iPhone users to update iOS now following ‘Darksword’ exploit discovery
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has urged Apple users to immediately update their iOS devices following the discovery of multiple security vulnerabilities. In a statement yesterday, MCMC said Apple had released an important security update addressing vulnerabilities, including an exploit chain known as “DarkSword” affecting iOS devices, particularly Apple iPhones. It said the vulnerabilities could be exploited together to compromise affected devices, potentially allowing unauthorised access and other malicious activities.

items.title: Police warn public after PD teen suffers ruptured eardrums, broken nose from modified firecrackers
items.summary: SEREMBAN, March 20 — A firecracker mishap last week left a 17-year-old boy with serious injuries, including ruptured eardrums in both ears and a broken nose in Bandar Springhill, Lukut in Port Dickson. Negeri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said the teenager also suffered injuries to his fingers and palms, as well as minor wounds on his face. “The Port Dickson district police headquarters received a report on the incident today at 6.17 pm.

items.title: Indian snacks ‘muruku’, ‘kacang putih’ become Hari Raya favourites
items.summary: IPOH, March 20 —Traditional Indian snacks like muruku and kacang putih are becoming popular Hari Raya gifts, as many Malay customers fall in love with their spicy and savoury flavours. While Malays continue to make their traditional kuih and festive treats for Hari Raya, muruku and kacang putih have emerged as must-have snacks during the celebrations. Roshileh Osman, 57, from Lenggong, Gerik, said this was her first time buying kacang putih in Buntong, although she had previously purchased similar snacks in her hometown.

items.title: The sky is not the limit, it’s just the beginning for these women in aviation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — The aviation sector has long been a male-dominated industry but women are boldly breaking through those barriers, making sacrifices to pursue their dreams, proving that passion and determination know no gender. It was reported that in 2021, women comprised 4.7 per cent of aircraft pilots globally, 3.1 per cent of aircraft maintenance engineers and 21.1 per cent of air traffic controllers, while in 2022, only six per cent of airline chief executive officers were women. Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG) said that as of March 6, 2026, women make up 36 per cent of total employees at the company.

items.title: Malaysia to celebrate Hari Raya Aidilfitri on Saturday
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 19 — Muslims in Malaysia will celebrate Hari Raya Aidilfitri on Saturday, March 21 this year. The announcement was made by Keeper of the Rulers’ Seal Tan Sri Syed Danial Syed Ahmad in a televised broadcast this evening. The announcement followed new moon sightings tonight at 29 locations nationwide.

items.title: Anwar congratulates Thailand’s Anutin on re-election, vows stronger bilateral ties
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has congratulated Thailand’s Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul on securing a renewed mandate to lead the country. In a Facebook post today, Anwar described Anutin as a “dear friend” and expressed his hopes for continued close engagement between the two leaders. According to international media reports, Anutin was re-elected prime minister today after securing a parliamentary majority.

items.title: Anwar hails NGO-led Malay-Indian reconciliation as sign of national maturity
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 19 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim described the reconciliation efforts being driven by non-governmental organisations (NGOs) representing the Malay and Indian communities as a reflection of the people’s maturity in choosing the path of peace and rejecting discord. He welcomed the reconciliation efforts, saying the initiative also uplifted the spirit of mutual respect between the races. Anwar also expressed his appreciation to all parties, including the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), who made the effort a success.

items.title: Heavy congestion hits highways out of Klang Valley as Hari Raya travel peaks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Traffic flow on major highways leading out of the Klang Valley is heavily congested this evening as vehicle volume surges ahead of the Hari Raya Aidilfitri holidays. She said traffic is also slow from Bukit Lanjan to Rawang, Taiping to Bukit Merah, Jawi to Bandar Cassia, and from Juru to Bertam. Meanwhile, traffic towards the east coast is slow-moving from the Gombak Toll Plaza to Bukit Tinggi, with congestion on the East Coast Expressway 2 (LPT2) from Cheneh to Chukai and around the Kuala Terengganu Toll Plaza.

items.title: Hong Kong national among four held in Damansara Heights ‘mini casino’ bust
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Four people, including a Hong Kong national, were arrested yesterday for allegedly operating an online gambling syndicate from a “mini casino” in the affluent Bukit Damansara neighbourhood. Kuala Lumpur police chief Commissioner Datuk Fadil Marsus said the three men and one woman, aged between 27 and 49, were detained during a raid conducted at 3.30 yesterday, according to Utusan Malaysia. The case is being investigated under Section 4(1)(g) of the Common Gaming Houses Act 1953 for operating a common gaming house, Fadil added.

items.title: Housing and Local Govt Ministry targets zero sick projects by 2030 under Madani housing reform, says Nga Kor Ming
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 19 — The Ministry of Housing and Local Government (KPKT) is stepping up efforts to strengthen governance in the real estate sector through its Madani Housing Reform agenda, with a target of achieving zero sick projects by 2030. “This Housing Reform aims to strengthen governance in the housing sector to achieve zero sick projects by 2030, while ensuring more Malaysians can own safe and livable homes,” he said in a statement today. Nga added that the Integrated Housing Monitoring System (HIMS) has been enhanced to enable real-time monitoring of project progress, financial flows and developers’ licence status.

items.title: Nirvana Asia suspects Cambodian scam syndicates behind urn theft-extortion plot
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Bereavement care giant Nirvana Asia Group has suggested that Cambodian scam syndicates may be responsible for the recent, organised theft of urns from memorial parks. The statement was issued after police confirmed a case involving 30 urns stolen from a memorial park in Nilai had evolved into an extortion attempt. Nilai district police chief Supt Johari Yahya said a suspect using a foreign WhatsApp number contacted the park's management to demand payment for the return of the cremated remains.

items.title: CM: Penang raises funding for mosque, surau development to RM25m this year
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 19 — The Penang government, through the Penang Islamic Religious Council (MAINPP), has allocated RM25.34 million this year for the development of mosques and surau across the state to strengthen religious institutions as centres of worship, education and community unity. “In line with the Penang Islamic Religious Development Agenda 2030 (APAI2030), which is based on the Vision Penang2030 framework in the field of Islamic education, Penang now has 352 Islamic schools and 222 Al-Quran and Fardu Ain (KAFA) classes registered under the Penang Islamic Religious Affairs Department (JHEAIPP). Chow pledged that the state leadership remains committed to strengthening the development of Islamic affairs as part of an inclusive state development agenda.

items.title: Penang establishes PIFC task force to position state as tech‑finance hub, white paper due 3Q, says CM
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 19 — Penang will establish a Special Task Force Committee to continue efforts to set up the Penang International Financial Centre (PIFC), based on a “whole-of-state” approach. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the decision, made during the state executive council meeting yesterday, was a step to strengthen the state’s commitment to realising the initiative. “I will chair the committee, while the State Secretary (Datuk Seri Zulkifli Long) will serve as deputy chairman, with members including the state financial officer, state legal adviser, representatives from the state economic planning unit (BPEN), Invest Penang, the Northern Corridor Implementation Authority (NCIA), the Malaysian Investment Development Authority (MIDA), and representatives from the private sector.

items.title: Couple killed in crash after car veers into opposite lane in Kota Tinggi, four others injured
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 19 — A couple were killed in an accident involving two vehicles at kilometre 77.3 of the Johor Bahru–Mersing road near Kota Tinggi this morning. Kota Tinggi district police chief Supt Yusof Othman said the victims were travelling in a Nissan Sentra, which collided with a Proton Saga carrying four people in the incident at about 9.45 am. “The victims, who were travelling from Mersing towards Johor Bahru, are believed to have entered the opposite lane and collided with the Proton Saga, which was travelling from Pasir Gudang to Kuantan,” he said in a statement.

items.title: Kota Kinabalu police warn of vehicle scam tactics as online fraud losses hit RM11.12m
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 19 — The Kota Kinabalu District Police Headquarters recorded losses totaling RM11.12 million from 214 online fraud cases between January and March 15. Acting Kota Kinabalu police chief Supt Syed Lot Syed Ab Rahman said these cases form part of the 290 commercial crime investigations opened by the Commercial Crime Investigation Division, with total losses amounting to RM14.628 million. He added that, besides online scams, 46 cases involved e-commerce fraud, particularly vehicle transactions, resulting in losses of RM328,888.

items.title: Madani govt ensuring sufficient supply of basic items, public urged not to panic buy, says Mustapha Sakmud
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 19 — The Madani Government will continue striving to ensure that the basic needs of the people are sufficient, especially in Sabah and Sarawak, in the face of current economic challenges, said Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Sabah and Sarawak) Datuk Seri Mustapha Sakmud. He said the measures included maintaining fuel prices and ensuring the supply of basic goods remained stable so that the people would not be burdened by the rising cost of living. But at the same time, the Madani Government will try to ensure our basic needs are sufficient.

items.title: Evening commute alert: Thunderstorms to hit KL, Putrajaya, Selangor, multiple other states
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued an urgent weather advisory for the Klang Valley and several other states, warning of heavy rain, thunderstorms, and strong winds expected to persist until 6pm today. According to the alert issued at 3.50 pm, the affected areas in Peninsular Malaysia include the entire Federal Territories of Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya, as well as the Selangor districts of Hulu Selangor, Gombak, Petaling, Hulu Langat, and Sepang. In the northern and central regions, residents in Kulim and Bandar Baharu in Kedah should prepare for adverse weather, alongside a significant portion of Perak, specifically Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang, and Muallim.

items.title: IGP defends handcuffing Zamri Vinoth, says procedure follows Sedition Act requirements
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Inspector-General of Police (IGP) Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said Zamri Vinoth was brought to court in handcuffs on Tuesday in accordance with legal procedures and regulations. He said Zamri’s initial arrest involved a case under Section 505 of the Penal Code, and he is also being investigated under the Sedition Act 1948, which requires a different procedural approach. Zamri, 42, whose full name is Muhammad Zamri Vinoth Kalimuthu, was charged at the Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court; Arun, 56, whose full name is Arumugam Dorasamy, was charged at the Jawi Magistrate’s Court; while e-hailing driver P. Mahendra Bhoopaty, 53, faced similar charges at the Seremban Magistrate’s Court.

items.title: IGP: Businessman Victor Chin linked to money laundering ‘matters’, currently abroad
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Police have linked businessman Victor Chin, a central figure in the ongoing “corporate mafia” probe, to activities under the Anti-Money Laundering Act (AMLA), Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said today. The IGP also disclosed that Chin, who has been openly critical of the investigation against him, is currently abroad as authorities seek international cooperation to advance their investigation, The Star reported. Separately, the IGP also commented on the police investigation into the international news agency Bloomberg, stating that several persons of interest in that case are also overseas.
